_comment: Data-driven style rules. Edit this file to ban/allow phrases without touching code. Patterns are JS regex source strings; flags are applied per pattern. Scope is the section the check runs against.

popularCultureScorecard._comment: The 'grading-rubric pretending to be prose' disease. Hard target. Scoped to popularCulture descriptions only — these MUST be lively interpretive prose, not a right/wrong checklist.

popularCultureWatch._comment: Softer scorecard tics that mutate from the banned set. Warn-level — flags the template before it hardens. 'captures'/'conveys'/'shows' only flagged as an evaluative hinge near a virtue noun.

allowlistStems._comment: SANCTIONED recurring framework phrases. The corpus repetition detector and template checks EXEMPT these — they are deliberate section architecture (Research/Educational scaffolding), not model tics. Per the user's correction: do NOT police these.

repetitionStems._comment: Corpus repetition detector config. Scans freeform interpretive sections for 4-5 word evaluative stems that recur across many entries and are NOT on the allowlist. This is the permanent anti-mole: it surfaces the NEXT tic by frequency, so you ban or allowlist it without knowing it in advance.
